result:
Done — the spec is fully implemented and the project builds cleanly (3 of 10 build cycles used).

I added the three missing operations to `CsvRow.calr`, alongside the existing `NeedsQuoting` (which I left untouched):

- **`EscapeField`** (`§F{f002}`, pure) — calls `NeedsQuoting`; if it holds, wraps the field in double-quotes with internal quotes doubled via `(replace field "\"" "\"\"")`, otherwise returns the field verbatim.
- **`JoinRow`** (`§F{f003}`, pure) — takes `List<str>`, escapes each field and joins with commas using a first-element flag; the empty list yields the empty string.
- **`SplitRow`** (`§F{f004}`, `§E{mut}` since it builds a list with `§PUSH`) — a character-index scan: commas flush the current field, a leading `"` enters quoted mode where doubled quotes contribute one literal quote and commas/CR/LF are content, and the final field (including one implied by a trailing comma) is pushed after the loop. The empty line falls through to a single empty field.

Two syntax details cost the first two build cycles: bare boolean variables can't be parenthesized as `§IF` conditions (the parser reads `(needs)` as an operator call), and `[str]` maps to `string[]` rather than the `List<string>` the spec pins — `List<str>` is the correct annotation, which I confirmed against the compiler's `TypeMapper`.

The generated C# signatures match the pinned public surface exactly (`bool NeedsQuoting(string)`, `string EscapeField(string)`, `string JoinRow(List<string>)`, `List<string> SplitRow(string)`). I also verified behavior end-to-end with a throwaway console project in /tmp (deleted afterwards, nothing added to the source tree): all targeted edge cases passed — empty line → one empty field, trailing comma, quoted commas/CR/LF, doubled quotes, `""` → empty field — plus a 20,000-case seeded round-trip fuzz of `SplitRow(JoinRow(fields))` with no mismatches.
